title: acer tetramerum var. betulifolium
intro: Acer tetramerum var. betulifolium, also known as the birch-leaf maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Sapindaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Acer tetramerum var. betulifolium include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions, such as acne and eczema. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
